  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c_iar", "name": "Institutional Alignment Readiness", "aliases": ["IAR"], "description": "A pre-deployment framework for assessing whether a target institution has the conditions needed to introduce, govern, and sustain a specific machine learning system.", "grounding_span": "Institutional Alignment Readiness", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "c_public_sector_ai", "name": "Public-sector AI", "aliases": ["public-sector artificial intelligence"], "description": "AI systems deployed in public institutions such as education, health, and social services.", "grounding_span": "public-sector artificial intelligence systems", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_deployment_readiness", "name": "Deployment readiness", "aliases": [], "description": "Readiness assessed at the point of deployment in public systems, distinct from model development readiness.", "grounding_span": "deployment readiness in public systems", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_artifact_evaluation", "name": "Artifact-level evaluation", "aliases": ["model-centered evaluation"], "description": "Evaluation of the model or dataset for technical suitability (accuracy, robustness, fairness, documentation, intended use).", "grounding_span": "Existing artifact-level evaluation re-", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "c_inst_op_compat", "name": "Institutional and Operational Compatibility", "aliases": [], "description": "IAR dimension: documented approval pathways, workflow fit, operator training, logistics, and timing fit with service calendars.", "grounding_span": "institutional and op-\nerational compatibility", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c_data_ecosystem", "name": "Data Ecosystem Maturity", "aliases": [], "description": "IAR dimension: dataset representativeness, data access and sharing protocols, annotation capacity, retention policy.", "grounding_span": "Data Ecosystem Maturity", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "c_human_oversight", "name": "Human Oversight Capacity", "aliases": [], "description": "IAR dimension: availability of qualified reviewers, override authority, referral pathways, and non-technical challenge mechanisms.", "grounding_span": "Human Oversight Capacity", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "c_fiscal_sustain", "name": "Fiscal Sustainability", "aliases": [], "description": "IAR dimension: budget clarity beyond pilot, maintenance and retraining plans, and continuity across funding or leadership transitions.", "grounding_span": "Fiscal Sustainability", "location": "Table 1"},
    {"id": "c_reg_alignment", "name": "Regulatory Alignment Readiness", "aliases": [], "description": "IAR dimension: privacy compliance, legal basis for collection and sharing, ethics review, consent, and contestability pathways.", "grounding_span": "Regulatory Alignment Readiness", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "c_resource_constrained", "name": "Resource-constrained settings", "aliases": [], "description": "Public institutions with limited resources, where gaps between technical viability and responsible deployment are most acute.", "grounding_span": "in resource-constrained settings.", "location": "Section 2.4"},
    {"id": "c_responsible_ai", "name": "Responsible AI", "aliases": [], "description": "Literature of principles, checklists, and documentation tools for evaluating AI systems and their development processes.", "grounding_span": "responsible AI literature", "location": "Section 2.1"},
    {"id": "c_sociotechnical", "name": "Sociotechnical critique of AI", "aliases": [], "description": "View that model behavior cannot be understood apart from the institutional settings in which systems are deployed.", "grounding_span": "sociotechnical critiques of AI", "location": "Section 2.2"},
    {"id": "c_ai_maturity", "name": "AI maturity models", "aliases": [], "description": "Instruments assessing whether an organization has the general capability to adopt AI.", "grounding_span": "AI maturity models assess whether an organization has", "location": "Section 2.3"},
    {"id": "c_trl", "name": "Technology Readiness Levels", "aliases": ["TRLs"], "description": "Frameworks evaluating the technical maturity of an artifact rather than institutional conditions.", "grounding_span": "(TRLs)", "location": "Section 2.4"},
    {"id": "c_referral_pathways", "name": "Referral pathways", "aliases": [], "description": "Institutional routes for acting on system outputs by qualified staff.", "grounding_span": "referral pathways", "location": "Section 2.3"},
    {"id": "c_hitl", "name": "Human-in-the-loop oversight", "aliases": [], "description": "Human review and override in the operation of a deployed AI system.", "grounding_span": "human-in-the-loop oversight.", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "c_provider_readiness", "name": "Provider readiness", "aliases": [], "description": "A companion notion to IAR concerning whether the developer or delivery team can maintain, retrain, and support the system over time.", "grounding_span": "provider readiness", "location": "Section 7"},
    {"id": "c_staging", "name": "Staging framework", "aliases": ["staging tool"], "description": "Use of IAR to support incremental, conditional deployment decisions rather than a binary go/no-go.", "grounding_span": "we use IAR as a staging framework.", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "c_deployment_stage", "name": "Deployment stage decision", "aliases": ["no-go", "pilot-only", "broader deployment"], "description": "Staging decisions such as not ready, internal validation, limited pilot, or broader deployment.", "grounding_span": "no-go, pilot-only", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_representativeness", "name": "Representativeness of training data", "aliases": [], "description": "Whether the training dataset adequately covers the target-population contexts.", "grounding_span": "representativeness of training data", "location": "Section 4.1"},
    {"id": "c_screening_vs_diagnosis", "name": "Screening rather than diagnosis", "aliases": [], "description": "Intended use of the case systems: supporting early identification and referral, not replacing clinical judgment.", "grounding_span": "screening rather than diagnosis", "location": "Section 4.1"},
    {"id": "c_design_reality_gap", "name": "Design-reality gap", "aliases": [], "description": "Gap between system design and operational reality endemic to IS deployment in resource-constrained settings.", "grounding_span": "design-reality gaps", "location": "Section 2.2"},
    {"id": "c_data_cascades", "name": "Data cascades", "aliases": [], "description": "Data failures in high-stakes AI that reflect upstream organizational conditions.", "grounding_span": "Data Cascades in High-Stakes", "location": "References"},
    {"id": "c_anthropometric", "name": "Image-based anthropometric screening", "aliases": ["Case A"], "description": "Computer-vision system estimating anthropometric indicators from photographs of early-grade learners.", "grounding_span": "Image-Based Anthropometric Screening", "location": "Section 4.1"},
    {"id": "c_speech_analysis", "name": "Speech analysis for early learning risk", "aliases": ["Case B"], "description": "System analyzing learners' oral reading and speech samples to identify early learning risk signals.", "grounding_span": "Speech Analysis for Early Learning Risk", "location": "Section 4.2"}
  ],
  "definitions": [
    {"id": "d_iar", "term": "Institutional Alignment Readiness", "verbatim_text": "We defineInstitutional Alignment Readiness(IAR) as a pre-\ndeployment framework for assessing whether a target insti-\ntution has the conditions needed to introduce, govern, and\nsustain a specific machine learning system.", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2026", "grounding_span": "We defineInstitutional Alignment Readiness(IAR) as a pre-\ndeployment framework for assessing whether a target insti-\ntution has the conditions needed to introduce, govern, and\nsustain a specific machine learning system.", "location": "Section 3"}
  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl_fail_at_deployment", "claim_text": "Many public-sector artificial intelligence systems fail not at the point of model development, but at the point of deployment.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Many public-sector artificial intelligence systems\nfail not at the point of model development, but\nat the point of deployment.", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "cl_bottleneck", "claim_text": "The bottleneck is rarely model quality alone.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "The bottleneck is rarely model quality alone.", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "cl_complement", "claim_text": "IAR is designed to complement, not replace, established AI evaluation tools.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "IAR is designed to complement,\nnot replace, established AI evaluation tools.", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "cl_five_necessary", "claim_text": "Each of the five dimensions included is independently necessary.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "each of the five dimensions included is inde-\npendently necessary", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "cl_fiscal_no_mechanism", "claim_text": "Fiscal Sustainability has no standard ML evaluation mechanism because budgetary continuity is not a property of a model.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Fiscal\nSustainability has no standard ML evaluation mechanism\nprecisely because budgetary continuity is not a property of a\nmodel.", "location": "Section 5"},
    {"id": "cl_tools_cannot_answer", "claim_text": "None of the common tools used in ML evaluation can, on their own, answer readiness questions such as whether required data are accessible at scale or whether qualified reviewers are available.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "none of the common tools\nused in ML evaluation can, on their own, answer readiness\nquestions", "location": "Section 5"},
    {"id": "cl_replication_required", "claim_text": "Cross-sector and cross-country replication by independent teams is required before IAR can be recommended as a general-purpose instrument.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "Cross-sector and cross-\ncountry replication by independent teams is required before\nIAR can be recommended as a general-purpose instrument.", "location": "Section 4.3"},
    {"id": "cl_nonredundant", "claim_text": "Completing a model card or a NIST RMF assessment for either operational case would have failed to surface the approval delays, referral gaps, or data-sharing constraints that determined their actual trajectories.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Completing a model card or a NIST RMF assessment\nfor either operational case would have failed to surface the\napproval delays, referral gaps, or data-sharing constraints\nthat determined their actual trajectories.", "location": "Section 6"},
    {"id": "cl_case_a_delay", "claim_text": "Expanding data collection to additional schools in Case A took more than six additional months because approval, coordination, and access had to be negotiated across multiple sites.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Expanding data collection to additional schools took substan-\ntially longer—more than six additional months—because\napproval, coordination, and access had to be negotiated\nacross multiple sites.", "location": "Section 4.1"}
  ],

  "proposed_relationships": [
    {"grounding_span": "IAR is intended\nto complement, rather than replace, existing responsible\nAI frameworks by making the receiving-context question\nexplicit.", "suggested_edge": "complements", "from_id": "f_iar", "to_id": "c_responsible_ai", "location": "Section 2.1"},
    {"grounding_span": "A direct extension of this work is to formalize a compan-\nion notion of provider readiness.", "suggested_edge": "companion_of", "from_id": "c_provider_readiness", "to_id": "c_iar", "location": "Section 7"},
    {"grounding_span": "IAR\noperationalizes this tradition by translating the sociotech-\nnical critique into a practical pre-deployment instrument", "suggested_edge": "operationalizes_tradition", "from_id": "f_iar", "to_id": "c_sociotechnical", "location": "Section 2.2"},
    {"grounding_span": "IAR addresses the readiness of thereceiving\ninstitution, not the totality of deployment readiness.", "suggested_edge": "assesses", "from_id": "f_iar", "to_id": "c_deployment_readiness", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"grounding_span": "existing artifact-level evaluation re-\nmains necessary, but IAR adds a second layer", "suggested_edge": "adds_layer_to", "from_id": "f_iar", "to_id": "c_artifact_evaluation", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"grounding_span": "the effort did not\nbegin in its final form; it emerged after an earlier project\ndirection proved infeasible because the required data were\nnot available.", "suggested_edge": "pivoted_due_to_data_constraint", "from_id": "c_speech_analysis", "to_id": "c_data_ecosystem", "location": "Section 4.2"}
  ]
}
